questions.question: In digital circuit design, what defines the "critical path"?
options: The shortest delay path from an input to an output.
The path with the highest number of gates.
The longest delay path from an input or register output to an output or register input.
The path that experiences the most crosstalk.
The path that consumes the most static power.

questions.question: In a multi-core processor system with shared memory, which of the following protocols is commonly used to maintain data consistency across multiple caches?
options: Virtual Memory Paging Protocol
Instruction Set Architecture (ISA)
Memory Read/Write Arbitration Protocol
MESI Protocol (Modified, Exclusive, Shared, Invalid)
Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS)

questions.question: In synchronous digital circuit design, what are "setup time" and "hold time" primarily concerned with?
options: The time required to power up and power down an IC.
The propagation delay through combinational logic gates.
Ensuring data stability at a flip-flop's input around the active clock edge.
The time taken for a processor to fetch an instruction from memory.
The delay introduced by interconnections (wires) on the chip.

questions.question: In advanced semiconductor manufacturing, what is the primary purpose of inserting "dummy metal fill" (or "density fill") structures into the integrated circuit layout?
options: To enhance the electrical conductivity of power lines.
To reduce parasitic capacitance between signal lines.
To improve the planarity of the wafer surface during chemical mechanical planarization (CMP).
To provide additional grounding points for noise reduction.
To reduce the overall chip area and cost.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary characteristic distinguishing a Reduced Instruction Set Computer (RISC) architecture from a Complex Instruction Set Computer (CISC) architecture?
options: RISC processors typically have a larger and more complex instruction set.
CISC processors prioritize single-cycle execution for most instructions.
RISC architectures generally employ simpler addressing modes and fixed-length instructions.
CISC architectures typically have more general-purpose registers than RISC.
RISC processors inherently support microcode for complex operations, while CISC does not.
